item.title: Wollongong researchers working to create shark-deterrent surfboard fins
item.description: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

item.title: Tom Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Elijah Hollands episode
item.description: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

item.title: Vanuatu denies it is about to sign a security deal with China as Australia works to ink its own pact
item.description: As Australia presses on to finally sign a key agreement with Vanuatu, reports of a security agreement with China have drawn a fierce response from Port Vila.

item.title: Two teenagers sentenced for roles in Ellenbrook school stabbing, planned as social media 'game'
item.description: Two boys are sentenced for their roles in the stabbing of a 15-year-old boy at a Perth high school, offending the prosecutor said was part of a "game" formulated online.

item.title: Former civil servant says there was political pressure to approve Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US
item.description: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Narco Islands: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Should wild deer be protected or declared a pest?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Man remains in critical condition with serious head injuries after 'violent incident' in Ngukurr
item.description: A man remains in a critical condition in hospital, and three others have been charged by police, after a "violent" altercation in the remote Northern Territory community of Ngukurr.

item.title: Government backs down on charging older Australians $50/hour for showers
item.description: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

item.title: Japan overhauls defence export rule to sell arms to 17 countries, including Australia
item.description: Japan has overhauled its decades-long defence export rule, allowing the country to sell lethal weapons internationally amid rising global tensions.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 22: We're off for a trip around the world
item.description: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

item.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
item.description: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

item.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
item.description: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

item.title: Butler urged to end 'limbo' for 500,000 Australians with psychosocial disability in NDIS reforms
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

item.title: Support services nationwide 'buckling under demand' during Sexual Assault Awareness Month
item.description: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

item.title: Closure of AEIOU centres for children with autism after NDIS cuts leaves lives upended
item.description: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

item.title: Junior clubs, former player and neuroscientists welcome new NRL guidelines to limit head knocks but some say it's not enough
item.description: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

item.title: Fourth man charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian
item.description: The 85-year-old's abduction triggered an 11-day search, with his remains later found near a golf course in Pitt Town, in the city's north-west.

item.title: FOI documents reveal Adelaide's COP31 considerations included gift bags and 'flotels'
item.description: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

item.title: A rare glimpse inside the services helping NSW women leaving custody get clean
item.description: Guthrie House is one of the few residential rehabilitation services in NSW designed to help women with current involvement with the criminal justice system get back on track.

item.title: Donald Trump extends Iran ceasefire but continues Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will hold off attacking Iran until Tehran's negotiating teams come up with a unified proposal for a peace deal.

item.title: What 100 years of Australian soil samples in our national archive can tell us about our history and future
item.description: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

item.title: Papunya residents living in extreme heat sue NT government over unsafe housing
item.description: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

item.title: Mark Butler announces major NDIS overhaul — as it happened
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler says the scheme was designed to support 410,000 people with a disability, but currently supports 760,000.

item.title: Kyle Flanagan benched as Dean Young names first Dragons NRL team, fires assistant coach Michael Ennis
item.description: Dean Young names the first team of his tenure as Dragons coach, dropping Kyle Flanagan to the bench and sacking assistant and attack coach Michael Ennis.

item.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace his Anzac relative's World War I footsteps
item.description: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

item.title: ASX tumbles as Cochlear shares crash 40pc to 10-year low — as it happened
item.description: One of Australia's biggest companies, Cochlear, has rattled investors by slashing its profit outlook, while the broader market drops.

item.title: Queensland Maroons name Tarryn Aiken, Ali Brigginshaw replacements for Women's State of Origin
item.description: With stalwart halves Ali Brigginshaw and Tarryn Aiken unavailable for selection, the Maroons name a new-look Queensland side for the first Women's State of Origin game in Newcastle.

item.title: Fire conditions on 'knife edge' in Queensland's south as burn window tightens
item.description: A Queensland Rural Fire Service superintendent says fire conditions in parts of the state are on the edge after very little rain this wet season.

item.title: Southern NSW family's push for affordable eczema medication so kids can have a normal childhood
item.description: The cost of relief for kids under 12 with severe eczema is $1,600 a month, but a push for PBS funding is looking to change that.

item.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting
item.description: US authorities are investigating if there was any criminal behaviour involving interactions between Open AI's ChatGPT app and a man accused of killing two people and injuring six others in a shooting at Florida State University.

item.title: Leicester City relegated to League One 10 years after Premier League title
item.description: Ten years after a fairytale Premier League title and five years since winning the FA Cup, Leicester City is destined for the third tier of English football.

item.title: Remote NT residents evacuated to Batchelor say security 'treating us like prisoners'
item.description: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

item.title: Meme warfare and 'slopaganda' are defining the AI-era Iran war
item.description: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

item.title: 'Terrifying' experience for Bunbury family as wild storm rips roof off their home
item.description: A woman describes her "terrifying" ordeal after a powerful cold front which sounded like a "jet plane" tore the roof off her family home while she was inside it on Monday night.

item.title: Boy, 15, among five people charged over alleged kidnapping of rapper's bodyguard in Sydney's west
item.description: Four men and a teenage boy are set to front court today over the alleged assault and kidnapping of a bodyguard for rapper and alleged Alameddine associate Ali Younes.

item.title: Newly revealed uncounted votes from SA election number 'in the order of dozens'
item.description: The number of newly revealed uncounted ballot papers from the March state election is believed to be in the "order of dozens", South Australia's Attorney-General says.

item.title: Three weeks of wages guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers in state, federal deal
item.description: A short-term rescue package announced by the Tasmanian and federal governments will guarantee the wages of workers at the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter.

item.title: About 100 GPs now qualified to diagnose ADHD in SA with calls to expand to regions
item.description: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

item.title: Meet the eco-venture turning European carp into popular dog treats
item.description: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

item.title: Australian golf junior hoping to test his skills at Canadian invitational
item.description: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

item.title: Carer charged over alleged sexual assault of woman with disability, police say
item.description: A 55-year-old man from Adelaide's west has been arrested and charged over the alleged sexual assault of a woman with a disability at Glenelg.

item.title: The sound of a night parrot recorded at a new location in southwest Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Critically endangered night parrots detected in south-west Queensland
item.description: Ecologists say thousands of hours of audio recordings have detected night parrot calls in south-west Queensland.

item.title: Michael Jackson gets the formulaic musical biopic he doesn't deserve
item.description: Spanning from the Jackson 5's 1960s discovery through to the height of his fame in the late 80s, Michael skates over the more challenging aspects of the King of Pop.

item.title: Timorese remember the child heroes who helped Anzacs in World War II
item.description: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

item.title: Deadly box jellyfish swarm recorded off Pilbara coast
item.description: Almost 100 deadly box jellyfish have gathered off a port in WA's north. Scientists say they likely belong to an unnamed species.

item.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers for SA
item.description: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

item.title: Jason Moloney eyeing top-level return, Conor Wallace still waiting for chance
item.description: Jason Moloney knows the path back to the summit of world boxing is paved with pain, but he will take another step back in Brisbane on Thursday night.

item.title: Millie Elliott returns, Teagan Berry to debut as NSW Sky Blues name Women's State of Origin I team
item.description: The Sky Blues welcome back Millie Elliott for the 2025 Women's State of Origin series opener, while Emma Verran's pregnancy opens up a spot for Teagan Berry to make her debut.

item.title: US commander says Australia is ready for AUKUS subs, warns of 'increasingly aggressive' China
item.description: The US's Indo-Pacific commander praises work to upgrade Australia's AUKUS base, but says he needs more naval firepower in the region as China rapidly expands its military.

item.title: After 27 years and 20 losses, can South Sydney finally find their first win in Melbourne?
item.description: The Rabbitohs have never won a game in Melbourne in almost three decades of trying but they might never get a better chance than this weekend.

item.title: Man accused of killing Labor identity Tim Picton pleads guilty over Kalamunda assault
item.description: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

item.title: More than 160,000 people to be kicked off NDIS as government overhauls eligibility test
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler has unveiled the government's plan to curb the ballooning growth of the NDIS and save the budget a projected $35 billion over four years.

item.title: Body found in New Zealand after deluge causes major flooding in Wellington
item.description: Police have found the body of a man as floodwaters recede in New Zealand's capital and the clean-up begins.

item.title: How these households divide and conquer mealtimes
item.description: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

item.title: New $30.5m funding package announced to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding
item.description: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

item.title: Assault charge against Offspring actor Alicia Gardiner dropped by Melbourne prosecutors
item.description: The actor was accused of grabbing the breast of a staffer at Parliament House in Melbourne during a pro-Palestine demonstration two years ago.

item.title: More than 3 million Australians over 65 to pay more for private health
item.description: The federal government argues the move will help re-establish fairness across the generations.

item.title: Tasmanian salmon industry launches campaign to get 'truth' out, amid calls for shutdown
item.description: A new advertising campaign featuring the stories of workers adjacent to Tasmania's fish farming industry is launched amid calls for the sector to be shut down.

item.title: NSW Public Guardian caseload likely took focus away from sick client, inquest hears
item.description: An inquest into the death of 54-year-old Raymond Wheatley heard his court-assigned guardian was juggling up to 60 other cases in the months leading up to his death.

item.title: How will the changes to the NDIS affect me? Five key takeaways from the government's planned overhaul
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler has unveiled sweeping cuts to the ballooning National Disability Insurance Scheme, aimed at cutting costs of the program down to $55 billion by the end of the decade.

item.title: CDU building more university student accommodation in Darwin, Casuarina with $70m loan
item.description: A towering 14-storey addition to Darwin's skyline is expected to ease housing stress for university students, with $70 million invested to tackle the city's competitive rental market.

item.title: Bombing of Darwin war veteran urges young people to learn about largest attack on Australia
item.description: At 101 years old, Bombing of Darwin survivor Terrance Byrne wants Australians to know exactly what unfolded during the World War II attack on Darwin — still the single largest foreign attack on Australian soil.

item.title: People with autism to be at centre of 160,000 NDIS removals
item.description: Minister Mark Butler announces his decision to remove more than 160,000 people from the national disability scheme over the next four years, with the timing labelled "troublesome" by autism advocates.

item.title: Resources companies fight gas tax push at parliamentary inquiry
item.description: An inquiry into the taxation of gas resources has heard from proponents for a tax, who believe Australians are being robbed of their fair share of wealth from the nation's natural resources, and opponents, who argue touching tax settings would spook investors.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Waste-to-energy plant proposed for Vuda Point in Fiji sparks fierce backlash
item.description: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

item.title: Fuel crisis making access to healthcare prohibitively expensive in regional areas
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Will Trump’s stoush with Pope turn US Catholics against the president?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Labor's NDIS savings plan hinges on state-run supports
item.description: Labor's plan to rein in NDIS growth will result in more than 160,000 people shifting off the scheme, with states expected to deliver alternative "foundational supports".

item.title: Woman's death at Darwin domestic violence shelter 'one of the worst days' of police officer's career, inquest hears
item.description: An inquest hears evidence from a senior police officer who ran into a room to disarm a mother who was self-harming inside a Darwin crisis accommodation facility, describing the incident as "one of the worst days" of her policing career.

item.title: EU backs $148bn loan for Ukraine after pipeline spat resolved
item.description: A bitter feud over an oil pipeline pitted Hungary against Ukraine and stalled the vitally needed funds for Kyiv.

item.title: First home buyer scheme found to be fuelling price increases at lower end of market, Cotality suggests
item.description: New research from property analytics firm Cotality appears to show that the Home Guarantee Scheme, intended to help first home buyers, may be worsening entry level affordability.

item.title: Tough NDIS budget decision could prove the exception — or the template
item.description: As far as tough budget decisions go, we haven't seen anything on this scale since Joe Hockey's ill-fated 2014 effort.

item.title: Ramones at 50: How a commercial flop became the foundation of punk rock
item.description: Fed up with where rock'n'roll was headed, these four New Yorkers changed the music world by playing sweet pop hooks at breakneck speed.

item.title: Major US firms granted access to Anthropic's Mythos to prepare against AI cyber threats, as Australian firms left out
item.description: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

item.title: More than a third of Australians seeking food relief are doing so for the first time, new survey finds
item.description: About one in three people accessing food relief services are doing so for the first time, according to a new report from Food charity OzHarvest.

item.title: Pacific leaders declared a fuel emergency but some didn't know about it. It's not clear what happens next
item.description: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

item.title: Victorian government announces $365m investment in CFA, forest firefighter upgrades
item.description: The Victorian government has unveiled its latest investment in CFA and forest firefighting upgrades.

item.title: Australian musicians claim they were ripped-off by 'predatory' booking agency
item.description: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

item.title: $7,000 NDIS cuts to social funding could reduce 'only interaction with outside world'
item.description: An average cut of $7,000 to NDIS participant plans could hurt social supports, providing the "only interaction with the outside world" for some people with disability, advocates warn, as the minister concedes the savings will have a material impact on participant plans.

item.title: Disability community 'scared' by dramatic NDIS changes as government defends overhaul
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler has defended the government's latest cost-cutting changes to the scheme in an interview with the ABC, amid an avalanche of participant concern.

item.title: Perth man jailed for importing heroin to Sydney in fold-out bed after being promised money
item.description: Barry James Calverley's lawyers argued he was an innocent drug mule who naively and gullibly trusted a story about why he needed to bring the bed to Australia.

item.title: Rescue package 'better than nothing', but uncertainty remains for Liberty Bell Bay workers
item.description: Three weeks of wages have been guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers, but with no buyer locked in, questions remain about what happens next.

item.title: A family's generational Anzac legacy and fight for compensation
item.description: No family in the world comes close to the fighting dynasty of Ricky Morris's. So, why are they still denied land owed generations ago?

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Ceasefire turns from fragile to chaotic, plus what else you should know
item.description: The countdown clock has been wiped. Perhaps it's a ploy or perhaps no-one knows what's going on, writes Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran.

item.title: SA's scandal-plagued Electoral Commission goes to ground in a crisis
item.description: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

item.title: Stump decorated as its remains left on Adelaide street for seven months
item.description: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

item.title: Mitch Marsh 50 not enough as Lucknow Super Giants fall to fourth straight Indian Premier League defeat
item.description: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

item.title: Bikie kingpin's private phone calls exposed in Mongols drug extortion case
item.description: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

item.title: PM says move to next fuel security level 'not imminent' — as it happened
item.description: Today's national cabinet meeting was "constructive", Anthony Albanese says, confirming that Australia remains at level 2 on its national fuel security plan. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Landholders band together against coal seam gas development to form Toprain Farming Collective
item.description: The Toprain Farming Collective is calling for greater protection of strategic cropping land in central Queensland, as Comet Ridge proposes an expansion and further development of the Mahalo Gas Hub.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina hits world ranking low after early Madrid Open exit
item.description: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

item.title: CBA to slash jobs — as it happened
item.description: The Commonwealth Bank is set to take the axe to more jobs. The union says employees are still reeling from the previous redundancy round.

item.title: Chelsea sack Liam Rosenior after just four months, Manchester City go top of Premier League with win at relegated Burnley
item.description: Chelsea sack Liam Rosenior after a desperate run of form saw them drop to seventh in the Premier League table, while Manchester City moved top following a 1-0 win at Burnley.

item.title: Blockchain billionaire Justin Sun takes Trump family’s crypto firm to federal court
item.description: A lawsuit alleges that World Liberty secretly installed tools to prevent the sale of Justin Sun's tokens after they became tradable in September 2025.

item.title: Study shows removing exposure to plastic from our food chain yields quick results
item.description: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

item.title: ABC's James Valentine swapped microphones — and found a new audience who loved him
item.description: James Valentine started his career on the stage, but it was his time behind the microphone in the studio and an interactive style that came to define him.

item.title: James Valentine, ABC talkback radio star, dies aged 64 after cancer diagnosis
item.description: The presenter and professional saxophone player is being remembered for his ability to explore the humour and oddities of daily life on the airwaves.

item.title: Petition against new McDonald's at Cowes, Phillip Island, receives 4,100 signatures
item.description: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

item.title: Netflix starts filming series Breakers in Western Australia's Wheatbelt
item.description: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

item.title: Alleged drink driver had child in ute when speeding at 151km/h, police say
item.description: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

item.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands
item.description: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

item.title: Bugler plays in more than 100 cemeteries to be pitch perfect for Anzac Day
item.description: Adam Farquharson feels the honour of Anzac Day every day as he plays to a different veteran's grave.

item.title: Mouse plague fears deepen as chemical regulator rejects CSIRO bait research
item.description: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

item.title: Australia's world-leading Wedgetail surveillance planes coming up for replacement, Defence planning reveals
item.description: Australia's fleet of world-leading surveillance aircraft is set to be replaced and analysts say the planes could be replaced by drones.

item.title: Hit Broadway musical Waitress comes down under starring Natalie Bassingthwaighte
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan sheds a tear ahead of long-awaited Gold Coast Suns debut
item.description: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan endured a year from hell in 2025, but things are looking up for him this season as he prepares to make his Gold Coast debut.

item.title: Luke Hemsworth on his new mixed martial arts film Beast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Charlize Theron and Taron Egerton on their film Apex set in the Australian bush
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stolen car in Kununurra
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Traumatised Kununurra residents call for help after car thefts and break-ins
item.description: Stolen cars and accommodation break-ins have led to growing frustration in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Authors ditch University of Queensland Press after Indigenous kids book pulled for 'anti-Semitism' of illustrator
item.description: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

item.title: Jackson Stacker inquest recommends his death be referred to homicide squad
item.description: The NSW state coroner recommends the death of van-packer Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be referred to the Unsolved Homicide Squad.

item.title: Bill adding new offences to adult crime, adult time laws passes Queensland parliament
item.description: Conspiring to commit murder, unlawful stalking and riot are among 12 new offences that juveniles can now be sentenced as adults for.

item.title: Leading Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup amid Pope Leo XIV attacks
item.description: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

item.title: Women on mission to preserve history of El Arish's soldier settlers
item.description: Anzac Day holds meaning for every Australian, but for Marie Carman and Lorelei King, it is another reminder of their deeply personal mission to uncover the forgotten stories of the soldiers who helped build their town.

item.title: Rebel Wilson mischaracterised 'innocent incident', defamation trial told
item.description: A young actor suing Rebel Wilson for defamation has given details in court about an incident at Bondi Beach, which she claims the Hollywood star has since mischaracterised.

item.title: Genesis Owusu releases new track Life Keeps Going while courting detractors
item.description: Genesis Owusu today releases his new track Life Keeps Going from his upcoming third album Redstar Wu & the Worldwide Scourge, as he encourages engagement with people who don't agree with his political views.

item.title: State and territory ministers react to the Albanese government's NDIS changes
item.description: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

item.title: No-grounds eviction debate splits WA tenants and landlords, as polling shows support for reform
item.description: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

item.title: Michaela Leonard to become Wallaroos' most-capped player in Anzac Day Test against New Zealand
item.description: Rugby was not on Michaela Leonard's radar when she was a child in Canberra, but she is about to achieve a historic milestone when the Wallaroos meet the Black Ferns on Anzac Day.

item.title: Woman faces murder charge over alleged hit-and-run that killed man at Hillier
item.description: The accused's lawyer today told the court that her client was one of four young females in a car that was being attacked when she attempted to flee.

item.title: Translating for Australian and US troops in Afghanistan made Sheraz a target for the Taliban, he's now got an Anzac Day moment
item.description: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

item.title: Indonesia's finance minister suggests Strait of Malacca toll before immediately backtracking
item.description: The suggestion of levying ships using the Strait of Malacca was suggested and then walked back by Indonesia's finance minister, but has prompted a response from Canberra.

item.title: Outlaw bikie gangs recruiting inside NT prisons
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Keiron Beau James Hedges charged with threatening to 'blow up' Perth Airport terminal
item.description: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

item.title: James Valentine made radio look easy. He was brave and generous on air and off
item.description: Children grew up with him, adults grew old with him, he kept us connected to our city, the world and each other. James helped us make sense of life and gave us permission to laugh at the absurdity of it all — and ourselves.

item.title: Trump 'always welcome' in Australia: Albanese on US ties amid war
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bulldogs offer update after star's horror fall as Curnow kicks a bag
item.description: Sydney's hold on top spot is strengthened by a 66-point win over the Western Bulldogs, whose injury woes continue.

item.title: Canavan's campaigning in Farrer proves a frosty foray
item.description: This week the Albanese government pushed out a major reform ahead of the federal budget, the Nationals leader was out in the cold on the campaign trail and a former Treasury secretary had some choice words about the push for gas tax reform.

item.title: Hannah Clarke's parents step back from charity due to personal toll
item.description: After their daughter and three grandchildren were murdered, Sue and Lloyd Clarke began a charity to improve awareness of coercive control. Now they've decided they need to step back for their own health.

item.title: Danish two-train collision injures at least 18
item.description: The incident happened north of Copenhagen, with images showing two yellow and grey trains, both with visible damage.

item.title: Russell Brand admits to having sex with 16-year-old when he was 30
item.description: British actor and comedian Russell Brand, who is facing multiple sexual assault charges, has admitted to having sex with a 16-year-old when he was 30.

item.title: David Malouf, esteemed Australian author, poet, essayist and librettist, dies aged 92
item.description: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

item.title: Garry Charles Kingston, husband of former WA Nationals MLC Louise Kingston, hit with child sex abuse charges
item.description: Former Nationals and Independent MLC Louise Kingston watched on as her husband Garry faced fresh child sexual abuse charges in a WA court.

item.title: Billionaire Judith Neilson's ex-staffer denied bail over $1.6m fraud allegations
item.description: Billionaire Judith Neilson's former secretary is accused of going on a $1.6m shopping spree using her employer's credit cards and bank information.

item.title: Grapevine berry inner necrosis virus officially detected for first time in multiple Australian states
item.description: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

item.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants
item.description: The government will not increase taxes on gas giants in next month's budget, echoing the industry's argument that exporters are already paying tens of billions of dollars in taxes each year.

item.title: 'Unusual' Adelaide warm weather sees residents soak up sun at beach
item.description: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

item.title: Fight over legal costs in Gina Rinehart's Hope Downs judgement begins in Perth
item.description: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining heiress Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgement in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

item.title: How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
item.description: Labor's plan to create a new "assessment tool" to determine future NDIS eligibility based on functional need could backfire without the right settings, say experts.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith may have been attempting overseas move, court documents say
item.description: Police were concerned war veteran Ben Roberts-Smith was attempting to move overseas and possibly to Singapore or Spain, court documents reveal.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Marshall fumes at NRL refs as Koroisau faces hip-drop tackle verdict
item.description: Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall has launched a passionate criticism of NRL officiating, following his side's 33-14 win over the Canberra Raiders.

item.title: How is there still a ceasefire as the US blockades the Strait of Hormuz and Iran seizes ships?
item.description: As part of its blockade of Iran, ships have been seized by US forces, others fired upon by Iran and, most recently, boarded by the regime's revolutionary guards. This is all while both sides maintain there is a ceasefire.

item.title: Lance Collard's 'crippling' ban reduced to four weeks with two suspended after St Kilda appeal
item.description: St Kilda successfully appeals the AFL's nine-week ban handed to forward Lance Collard over a homophobic slur, reducing it to four weeks with two suspended.

item.title: Can British PM Keir Starmer survive the Mandelson scandal?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: This ANZAC Day, interpreters like Sheraz are being recognised for their service
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Final day of inquest into death of Toko-Harieta Maki hears from top police, child safety representatives
item.description: A coronial inquest hears that Northern Territory authorities assessed that a father posed "no risk" to his child, despite allegations he repeatedly abused his partner and shook the baby.

item.title: Hackers steal millions from Sri Lanka's finance ministry destined to repay Australia
item.description: Cyber criminals have hacked Sri Lanka's finance ministry, stealing millions of dollars destined to repay Australia.

item.title: Live: Trump says ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel extended for three weeks
item.description: Donald Trump says the US will work with Lebanon to "protect itself from Hezbollah" and that he will meet with Benjamin Netanyahu soon. Follow live.

item.title: Four 'finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices amid regulator crackdown
item.description: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

item.title: Viva Energy boss says more refineries needed for secure fuel supply
item.description: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

item.title: Four charts that paint a worrying picture for global fertiliser prices
item.description: Farmers around the world are going to be paying more for one of their most important inputs, fertiliser. Here are four charts that explain why.

item.title: NDIS cuts leave participants concerned about what lies ahead
item.description: NDIS participants set up their lives around the scheme's rules. But now the rules are changing and they fear what lies ahead.

item.title: NDIS fraud crackdown will flip rules on their head for 'let it rip' provider market
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

item.title: Baby boomers beware: Labor is reading the room on generational inequity
item.description: The Albanese government is reading the room when it comes to intergenerational equity ahead of the May 12 budget.

item.title: Britain bracing for prospect of sixth PM in seven years, as some say Keir Starmer's leadership 'now terminal'
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

item.title: Power price warning as proposed capital gains tax changes create 'uncertainty' for foreign investors
item.description: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

item.title: Today in history, April 24: Jane Fonda's important place in fitness industry thanks to workout video
item.description: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Matildas goalkeeper Chloe Lincoln on finding her voice and commanding her idols
item.description: The A-League Women finals kick off this weekend, with Brisbane to make its first appearance in five years.

item.title: These fishermen in the South China Sea get paid for their time on water — not to fish
item.description: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

item.title: Wallaroos ready to face New Zealand in Anzac Day Test after wild weather on United States tour
item.description: Having survived the threat of tornadoes during their US tour, the Wallaroos are enjoying a distraction-free build-up to their Anzac Day Test against New Zealand.

item.title: When is the Anzac Day public holiday? Which states have Monday off?
item.description: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

item.title: Tasmania Police sergeant used dispatch system in 'relentless' monitoring of wife in family violence matter
item.description: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

item.title: Sikh soldiers leave a legacy that hides in plain sight each Anzac Day
item.description: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

item.title: Anzac Day is on Saturday. What is open in Queensland and where are services being held?
item.description: More than 500 events will be held across Queensland to commemorate Anzac Day on Saturday. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Everything to know about the dawn service and retail trading hours for Anzac Day in NSW
item.description: From when to catch the dawn service to what shops will be open, here is everything you need to know about the Anzac Day long weekend in NSW.

item.title: US Department of Justice watchdog announces Epstein files audit
item.description: The audit follows months of controversy over censorship, missed deadlines and the publication of survivors' details.

item.title: Adelaide Festival appoints new Writers' Week director in wake of crisis
item.description: The new director of Adelaide Writers' Week says she is hopeful "really important lessons have been learned" following the implosion of this year's literary festival.

item.title: Figure skating teenager Hana Bath is making history for Australia
item.description: At just 15, Hana Bath became the first Australian female to ever land a triple axel in competition. The rising star now has her eye on the 2030 Winter Olympics.

item.title: Fuel prices in Australia: how rising costs are forcing Australians to change their habits
item.description: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Israel, Lebanon peace talks, Trump's 'shoot and kill' order, and what else to know
item.description: Talks in Washington are set against a backdrop of decades-long disputes and deep mistrust, but at least they're happening.

item.title: Adam Walton earns big Madrid Open victory, Alexei Popyrin's 2026 season struggles continue
item.description: Queenslander Adam Walton has enjoyed a fine win at the Madrid Open to arrest a miserable sequence of Aussie losses at the clay-court event.

item.title: Firefighters trained as community first responders revive man after heart attack
item.description: A heart attack survivor says a program set up to improve the chance of survival for patients in isolated NSW communities is the reason he is alive.

item.title: Rockhampton landmark The Criterion Hotel for sale for first time in 35 years
item.description: From hosting royalty to stars popping in for a steak, the hotel has a spooky past with tales of a jilted lover.

item.title: Live: Gas giants get resource for free? That's 'tosh', says King
item.description: Resources Minister Madeleine King has taken aim at those who say gas giants are obtaining the commodity for free, saying she's "mystified as to how they get away with such tosh". Follow live.

item.title: Regional providers, families fear they will be hit hardest by cuts to NDIS
item.description: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

item.title: Australia's most successful Winter Olympics team in history honoured at Snow Australia awards
item.description: Australia's Winter Olympians of the 2026 Milano-Cortina Games, which won a record six medals, have been honoured at the Snow Australia Awards.

item.title: Alison didn't recognise the symptoms of her stroke. She wants to stop others making the same mistake
item.description: When Alison Reidy, then 38, woke in terrible pain, she had no idea she was having a stroke. Now she's trying to improve stroke awareness in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Appeal board says homophobic and racist language commonplace in AFL due to 'highly competitive' nature of the game
item.description: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

item.title: Live: Oil prices spike again just as Australian fuel prices drop
item.description: The price of unleaded petrol is back to just 10 cents a litre more than before the war, even as oil prices are spiking again on global markets. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Victoria Cross recipient Keith Payne calls for federal government to step up veteran support
item.description: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

item.title: Regional RSLs across Victoria facing tough times ahead of Anzac Day
item.description: One RSL sub-branch is down to just three members and is worried about succession, but it is not alone in its troubles.

item.title: Parents fear for child safety as Dunolly Primary School crossing supervisor axed
item.description: Parents at a Victorian school are calling for a crossing supervisor to be reinstated along a busy road, saying it is a matter of time until a child is killed.

item.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine
item.description: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

item.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
item.description: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

item.title: Wondering what to read next? The 10 best books out in April
item.description: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

item.title: Jason Moloney, Conor Wallace and Max McIntyre win gruelling contests on blood-soaked night in Fortitude Valley
item.description: Former bantamweight world champion Jason Moloney earned a bloody victory over awkward American Andre Donovan to retain his IBF intercontinental and WBC Australasian bantamweight straps, as Max McIntyre and Conor Wallace also kept themselves in the picture for higher honours.

item.title: Two people arrested after $13m drug seizure on Hume Highway
item.description: A man and woman in their 20s are expected to face court after allegedly being found with 33 kilograms of methylamphetamine.

item.title: Law firm ordered by NSW Supreme Court to pay Broken Hill council more than $4.7m
item.description: The NSW Supreme Court has ordered a Sydney-based law firm and related companies to pay the Broken Hill City Council more than $4.7 million.

item.title: Mapping the most critical shipping lanes in the world
item.description: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

item.title: Teen killer of Queensland grandmother Vyleen White loses sentence appeal
item.description: The 16-year sentence handed to the teenager who murdered south east Queensland woman Vyleen White has been upheld by the state's Court of Appeal.

item.title: Whale rescuers prepare for increased entanglements during annual migration
item.description: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

item.title: Sick kangaroos isolated by urban growth on NSW Mid North Coast
item.description: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

item.title: New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel to sit out NFL Draft as he seeks counselling
item.description: The New England Patriots say they support head coach Mike Vrabel's decision to miss the final day of the NFL Draft to seek counselling following publication of photos with journalist Dianna Russini.
